repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3 PartialID catalogue placeholder: generic Manski-family bounds and LP-style partial-ID entries expected, but no long-term-surrogate entry

published_prior_art.cite: Manski1990: finite bounded-outcome partial-ID bounds by optimizing over compatible latent distributions
AtheyChettyImbensKang2019: surrogate-index identification under Prentice-style surrogacy and comparability

repo_prior_art.cite: q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends: aggregate pretrend summaries lose latent information; pid_dynamic_iv_compliance: memory-retaining dynamic IV LP bounds

published_prior_art.cite: FanManzanaresParkQi2026 and HuangWangYuanZhaoZhang2026 are closest but do not give noisy-panel drift-aware strict tightening
ChenGengJia2007, VanderWeele2013, and MaYinLiuGeng2021 address surrogate-paradox sign criteria, not noisy-panel drift transport endpoints

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The endpoint-extension threshold kappa_b charges only treated-arm drift U1(1)->UH(1), but an ATE endpoint can fail to extend through either treated or control arm drift.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The two-by-two transport formulas use m(v) as the post-drift outcome response in both arms, but §7 only states a control/no-drift bridge curve and should explicitly link Y_H(d) to m(U_H(d)).

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine kappa_b as an endpoint-extension threshold over both arms, or add a precise argument that only the treated arm can bind for the stated terminal-only comparison.

Add an explicit bridge-response assumption for the transport conjecture: conditional mean m(v) applies to Y_H(d) once the post-surrogate state is U_H(d)=v, without imposing Prentice surrogacy on observed S_J.
Add a boundary corollary showing that when rho exceeds both endpoint-extension thresholds, the full-panel and terminal-only intervals coincide.

recommended_next_step: Revise the arm-indexing of kappa_b and the m(v) outcome-response assumption, then rerun novelty review.
